title: nepeta nuda subsp. caerulea
intro: Nepeta nuda subsp. caerulea, also known as dwarf blue catmint, is a perennial flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and soothing respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Nepeta nuda subsp. caerulea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
